'''Damien Blanch''' (born 24 May 1983) is a former [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer. He last played in the [[Super League]] as a {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Catalans Dragons]], and has previously played for the [[Widnes Vikings]], [[Castleford Tigers]] ([[List of Castleford Tigers players|Heritage No. 843]]) and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] ([[List of Wakefield Trinity players|Heritage No. 1263]]).<ref name="Blanch signs deal with Wakefield">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/7037611.stm|title=Blanch signs deal with Wakefield|publisher=BBC|date=2007-10-10|access-date=2007-10-10}}</ref>

==Background==
Blanch was born in [[Sydney]], New South Wales, Australia.

==Career==
He received an international call-up from [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] in November 2006,<ref name="Damien Blanch">{{cite web|url=http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=2|title=Damien Blanch|publisher=Widnes|date=2007-07-19|accessdate=2007-07-19|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20070927201434/http://www.widnesvikings.co.uk/players.php?id=2|archivedate=27 September 2007|url-status=dead }}</ref> and was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#Ireland|Ireland]] squad for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="Cassidy included in Ireland squad">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/7657596.stm|title=Cassidy included in Ireland squad|publisher=BBC|date=2008-10-07|access-date=2008-10-07}}</ref> He finished the tournament among the top try scorers, attracting praise from many pundits for his good performances as Ireland exceeded expectations.

He was named [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] player of the year for 2009.<ref name="Hayne named Player of the Year">{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/hayne-named-player-of-the-year/story-e6frexnr-1225795997992|title=Hayne named Player of the Year|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|date=2009-11-10|accessdate=2010-04-19}}</ref>

Blanch joined Catalans Dragons ahead of the 2011 Super League season, and remained there for three years before departing at the end of 2013.<ref>{{cite news 
|title = Dragons snap up Blanch
|publisher = Sky Sports
|date = 2010-09-13
|url = http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12216_6374532,00.html
|accessdate = 2010-09-14}}</ref> In 2011 he was the club's top try-scorer.

He was named in the Ireland squad for the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup]].

He is currently playing for the Thirroul Butchers in the Illawarra Coal League.
